question: How do you read and write Parquet, Delta, and CSV files in PySpark with key options?
answer:
Reading:
  # Parquet (schema auto-inferred, column pruning and predicate pushdown built-in)
  df = spark.read.parquet('s3://bucket/data/')
  df = spark.read.parquet('s3://bucket/data/', mergeSchema=True)  # schema evolution

  # Delta Lake
  df = spark.read.format('delta').load('s3://bucket/delta-table/')
  df = spark.read.format('delta').option('versionAsOf', 5).load(...)  # time travel

  # CSV with options
  df = spark.read.csv('s3://bucket/data.csv', header=True, inferSchema=True,
                      sep=',', quote='"', escape='\\', nullValue='NULL')

Writing:
  # Parquet
  df.write.mode('overwrite').partitionBy('year', 'month').parquet('s3://bucket/out/')

  # Delta
  df.write.format('delta').mode('append').partitionBy('date').save('s3://bucket/delta/')

  # CSV (avoid for large data)
  df.write.mode('overwrite').option('header', True).csv('s3://bucket/out/')

Write modes: overwrite, append, ignore, errorIfExists

Key options: partitionBy for hive-style partitioning, repartition() before write to control file count, coalesce(1) to write a single file (only for small data).
